methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $1.5B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $600M - $4.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Ultima Genomics
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series C), post-money $4B

private.name: Insitro
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series C), post-money $2.4B

data.narrative:
Colossal Biosciences is a Series C-stage biotech company currently valued at $1.5B post-money, having raised $225M+. The valuation assessment employs a peer set of 9 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies ranging from $600M to $4.0B, supplemented by three public biotech comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) with a median P/S multiple of 7.4x.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement, yielding $2.5B. Peer-median analysis across the nine private comparables—including Ultima Genomics at $4.0B, Insitro at $2.4B, and HistoSonics at $2.2B—produces an implied valuation of $1.8B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied; target revenue is not disclosed, preventing P/S application despite the sector median of 7.4x.

The two-method triangulation produces a median estimate of $2.2B, with a low-end floor of $1.5B (current post-money) and a high-end ceiling of $2.9B. The $700M spread reflects material uncertainty around stage-progression rate and peer median weighting. Key unresolved variables include target-company revenue disclosure (needed for public comp triangulation), clinical/regulatory pathway clarity, and capital efficiency relative to the peer cohort. The current $1.5B valuation sits at the conservative end of the supported range.
